name: Kansas City Steak & Spinach Salad
recipeIngredient: 3/4 lb Boneless tender beef steak - about 3/4 inch thick
1 lb Fresh spinach (washed, torn into bite-size pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the steak in a large plastic bag.
2. Mix 2 tablespoons lite soy sauce, 1 tablespoon vinegar, garlic, and pepper with the steak in the bag.
3. Press out as much air as possible from the bag, then seal it tightly.
4. Turn the bag over several times to coat the steak on both sides thoroughly.
5. Marinate for 30 minutes, turning the bag once.
6. In a separate bowl, combine the remaining lite soy sauce, vinegar, oil, and oregano.
7. Preheat the broiler.
8. Broil the steak for 4 minutes on each side (for rare), or until it reaches your desired doneness. Once done, cut across the grain into thin slices.
9. In a large bowl, combine the sliced steak, remaining ingredients, and blend the soy sauce mixture.
10. Pour the dressing over the spinach mixture and toss to combine.
11. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
